IS2.name: 'Civilight Eterna'
IS2.description:
All enemies take +150% True damage.
Most enemy self-damage effects and most environmental effects (survivalist, Originium altars etc.) that can damage enemies are implemented as true damage, which Civilight Eterna will amplify. For example, Enraged Possessed Soldiers take 350 true damage/second by default, and Civilight Eterna will amplify that to 825/second. For the “Chalice of Regret” enemy, which absorbs 95% of the damage taken by enemies in its range, and transfers it to itself as true damage, its damage transferred to itself is amplified by Civilight Eterna.
For true damage effects that are capable of hitting both enemy and allied units, Civilight Eterna will only amplify the damage if it affects an enemy.

IS5.description:

While not officially a King of Sarkaz relic, it is practically a requirement to make them viable. In IS5, the last point of Max Life gained from command level is at command level 6; if you take Visage before command level 6, leveling up will ruin its synergy with King's and King of Sarkaz's sets.

IS2.name: Hand of Choker
IS2.description:
Heavyshooter, Deadeye, and Besieger Operators' attacks will defeat all non-Boss enemy units at 30% or less HP.
If a shot's damage brings an enemy's HP below the insta-kill threshold, the enemy will be insta-killed by that shot.

IS3.name: Hand of Choker
IS3.description:

If a shot's damage brings an enemy's HP below the insta-kill threshold, the enemy will be insta-killed by that shot.

IS4.name: Hand of Outburst
IS4.description:
Heavyshooter, Deadeye, and Besieger Operators deal increasing damage to enemies as the target's HP decreases, up to a maximum of 50%, and instantly defeat enemies below 20% HP.
If a shot's damage brings an enemy's HP below the insta-kill threshold, the enemy will be insta-killed by that shot.

IS5.name: Hand of Fireworks
IS5.description:

The AOE range of the extra projectile has an approximate radius of 0.5 tiles.

IS5.name: Fearless Blade

IS5.name: Blood Tax Mystery

IS5.name: Suspicion Mystery

IS5.name: Fabrication Mystery

IS5.name: Sigil Mystery

IS5.name: Tranquil Mystery
IS5.description:
All allied and enemy units have -25% ATK; only active in Bizarre Fragments.

IS5.name: Silk Bond Mystery

External modifier, stacks additively with other Judgement relics.

IS2.name: 'Flowers of Quinde'
IS2.description:
All friendly units recover 10 HP every second.
The "Art Connoisseur" encounter can only show up when you have at least one of 'Flowers of Quinde' or 'Numbness and Obscenity'.
Having both will allow you to obtain 'Beauty and Ugliness in the Age of Terra'.

IS2.name: 'Numbness and Obscenity'
IS2.description:
All friendly units have +5 RES.
The "Art Connoisseur" encounter can only show up when you have at least one of 'Flowers of Quinde' or 'Numbness and Obscenity'.
Having both will allow you to obtain 'Beauty and Ugliness in the Age of Terra'.

IS2.name: 'Brokenblade'
IS2.description:
All Operators have -30% Max HP, but have -50% Redeployment Time.
The way -redeployment time% effects are calculated is that % reductions are applied first, then flat reductions.

IS3.name: 'Wavebreaker'
IS3.description:
All Operators have -25% HP, but have -50% Redeployment Time.
The way -redeployment time% effects are calculated is that % reductions are applied first, then flat reductions.

IS2.name: 'Whiteflower'
IS2.description:
When the battle begins, randomize all allied operators' Deployment Cost.
The effect that shuffles DP costs can return an operator's own DP cost to itself.

IS3.name: Vivaparous Lily
IS3.description:
When a battle begins, reduces the DP Cost of all Operators by 3, then scrambles their DP Cost.
The effect that shuffles DP costs can return an operator's own DP cost to itself.

IS2.name: 'Focus'
IS2.description:
Whenever you deploy a unit, deal 3000 Arts damage to a random enemy and the area around it.
The definition of "friendly unit" is very broad and encompasses the vast majority of deployables.

IS3.name: 'Sui's Wrath'
IS3.description:
Whenever you deploy a unit, deal 3000 Arts damage to a random enemy and the area around it.
The definition of "friendly unit" is very broad and encompasses the vast majority of deployables.

IS4.name: 'Sui's Wrath'
IS4.description:
Whenever you deploy a unit, deal 3000 Arts damage to a random enemy and the area around it.
The definition of "friendly unit" is very broad and encompasses the vast majority of deployables.

IS3.name: Derivative Terminal - Supercharged Mod
IS3.description:
Summons have +30% HP, +30% ATK, and +30 ASPD.
This applies to many deployable units that you might not initially think of as summons, for example Pozyomka's typewriter.

IS3.name: Distant Home's Guide
IS3.description:
Designates one deployable tile at the beginning of battle; friendly units deployed on that tile will recover 1 SP and 3% Max HP per second.
This collectible can choose some tiles that are not actually deployable on.

IS3.name: Abandoned Banner
IS3.description:
Designates one deployable tile at the beginning of battle; friendly units deployed on that tile have +50% ATK and +50 ASPD.
This collectible can choose some tiles that are not actually deployable on.

IS3.name: 'Glory Pack'
IS3.description:
When an Operator is defeated, it will Stun all enemies in the four adjacent tiles for 6 seconds.
Contrary to the description, the effect triggers whenever an operator or a summon dies, and enemies are stunned in a 1.25 tile radius centered on the unit.

IS5.name: 'Glory Pack'
IS5.description:
When an Operator is defeated, it will Stun all enemies in the four adjacent tiles for 6 seconds.
Contrary to the description, the effect triggers whenever an operator or a summon dies, and enemies are stunned in a 1.25 tile radius centered on the unit.

IS4.name: Palmtop Pavilion
IS4.description:
All Operators gain +1 Block, and an additional +1 Block after being deployed for more than 60 seconds.
With the exception of Liberator Guards, this will turn 0 block units into 1 block units, which is generally undesirable.

IS4.name: Northwind Construct
IS4.description:
[Machine] and [Arts Creations] enemies will be immediately Frozen with their first attack for 15 seconds.
This will generally screw you over in Manmade Carnival more than it helps. Be careful about picking this collectible.

IS4.name: Paradigm Apparatus
IS4.description:
All enemies have ASPD -30 when on tiles affected by Dominion.
Capable of desyncing the Liberator cycle on 4th ending. Consider skipping if going for 4th ending.

IS4.name: Cloud-Moving Totem
IS4.description:
All enemies have -15% Movement Speed, and the Weightless effect further drops their Weight Level by 2.
Capable of desyncing the Liberator cycle on 4th ending. Consider skipping if going for 4th ending.

IS4.name: Rusted Blade - Disturbance
IS4.description:
For every Specialist Operator on the field, all enemies have -5% Movement Speed (stacks up to 8 times).
Capable of desyncing the Liberator cycle on 4th ending. Consider skipping if going for 4th ending.

IS5.name: Rusted Blade - Protraction
IS5.description:
For every Specialist Operator on the field, all enemies have -5% Movement Speed (stacks up to 8 times).
Affects boat jump timing.

IS4.name: Celestial Dust
IS4.description:
Ranged Operators Levitate all enemies within attack range for 2 seconds when a skill is manually activated.
The levitate is applied before the skill, which can cause complications with certain operators.

IS4.name: Ring of Thorns
IS4.description:
When Melee Operators are healed, they and Operators in the adjacent 4 tiles will recover 1 SP.
The SP gain affects not just operators, but all friendly units including summons and devices.
Any healing effect that produces green healing numbers will trigger this.

IS4.name: Clairvoyant's Reveal
IS4.description:
The DP Cost and Redeployment Time of all Operators is randomly changed.
Each operator gets their DP cost and redeployment time set randomly to somewhere within the range of [40% of its base value, 120% of its base value].

IS4.name: Scarred Amber
IS4.description: Uncaps Anti-Interference Index Limit, and every surplus index point will grant all allied units HP +8% and ATK +8%.

IS4.name: Roundstone Altar
IS4.description:
There is a chance after battle to permanently increase all allied units' ATK and DEF by 5% (Can stack up to 10 times).
There is a 30% chance of gaining a stack after each combat.

IS5.name: Roundstone Altar
IS5.description:
There is a chance after battle to permanently increase all allied units' ATK and DEF by 5% (Can stack up to 10 times).
There is a 30% chance of gaining a stack after each combat.

IS4.name: Living Woodplate
IS4.description:
All allied melee units gain a Barrier equal to 50% of their Max HP on deployment.
The barrier can mitigate the 30%/60% of HP loss caused by the injury/crisis trigger collapsal paradigms.
The 50% shield is based off the HP of the operator after external buffs are applied but before internal buffs.

IS5.name: Living Woodplate
IS5.description:
All allied melee units gain a Barrier equal to 50% of their Max HP on deployment.
The barrier can mitigate the 30%/60% of HP loss caused by the injury/crisis trigger collapsal paradigms.

IS3.name: Fincatcher's Shawl
IS3.description:
Ranged Operators gain Camouflage.
This provides camouflage, not invisibility — units can still take damage from most AoE effects.
This applies to units tagged as "ranged" in the in-game tag system.

IS4.name: Finshell Shield
IS4.description:
All allied ranged units gain 3 layers of Shield on deployment.
1 shield can mitigate the 30%/60% HP loss caused by the injury/crisis trigger collapsal paradigms.

IS3.name: Medicine Sticks
IS3.description:
All allied units gain 2 layers of Shield when deployed.
Despite the description specifying operators, this grants shields to non-operator units that can take damage too, like summons or devices.

IS5.name: Medicine Sticks
IS5.description:
All allied units gain 2 layers of Shield when deployed.
Despite the description specifying operators, this grants shields to non-operator units that can take damage too, like summons or devices.

IS4.name: Empty Fowlbeast
IS4.description:
Summons have +30% HP, +30% ATK, and when on the field, grant +60% ATK to their summoners.
Applies to a surprisingly large number of things beyond ordinary summons, including trapmaster mines, Ulpian's S3 return point, Wis'adel's shadows, Ebenholz's S2 goats, and more.

IS5.name: Empty Fowlbeast
IS5.description:
Summons have +30% HP, +30% ATK, and when on the field, grant +60% ATK to their summoners.
Applies to a surprisingly large number of things beyond ordinary summons, including trapmaster mines, Ulpian's S3 return point, Wis'adel's shadows, Ebenholz's S2 goats, and more.

IS4.name: Lakebed Aegis
IS4.description:
Shield +1; all allied units gain Max HP +60% if you have any Shield at the start of a battle.
Unusually, this is implemented as an internal buff, meaning the +60% HP is multiplicative with most other +HP% collectibles.
The effect is granted as long as you had at least 1 shield when the operation starts.

IS5.name: Lakebed Aegis

IS2.name: 'Unleashings'
IS2.description:
All Operators recover 1 SP every time they take damage or Elemental Injury.
The effect triggers when they lose life — attacks, self-induced HP loss, Poison Mist, environmental hazards, etc.

IS2.description:
Enemies take increased damage from allied units as range increases (max 100%).
The damage amplification is approximately: 20% * (distance - 1), scaling from 1 tile to 6 tiles.
The damage amplification is a final modifier, not an ATK% multiplier, so it does not help penetrate defense.

IS3.description:
Enemies take increased damage from allied units as range increases (max 100%).
The description in IS3 incorrectly claims it maxes out at 50%, but it maxes out at 100% like every other IS.
The damage amplification is a final modifier, not an ATK% multiplier, so it does not help penetrate defense.

IS5.name: 'Bridge of Knowledge'
IS5.description:
Every Inspirer used grants all allied units +5% HP (Stacks up to 10 times).
Just using the inspirer is enough for a stack, you don't need to bring it into a combat.

IS5.name: Cursed Counterbeast
IS5.description:
All Operators gain +5 ASPD; consumes 1 Inspirer after each battle to grant an additional 5 ASPD (stacks up to 10 times).
Consumes Inspirers only while under 10 stacks. Prioritizes consuming inspirers by: lowest rarity > highest toil > first obtained.

IS5.name: Doodle of Hope
IS5.description:
Hope +3; Emergency Ops will no longer appear when refreshing nodes; triggers wondrous effects in Encounter nodes.
Unlocks special options in many encounters. Exchanging Doodle of Hope at a Lost and Found node gives you Talons of Hatred.

IS5.name: Talons of Hatred
IS5.description:
All allied units and enemies have +20% ATK; Emergency Ops will appear more often when refreshing nodes; triggers wondrous effects in Encounter nodes.
Unlocks options to do Emergency versions of specific combats in encounters. Exchanging Talons at a Lost and Found node gives you Doodle of Hope.

IS5.name: Lamp of Wishes
IS5.description:
Originium Ingots +10, Wish Fulfilled will appear more often when refreshing nodes; triggers wondrous effects in Wish Fulfilled.
Lamp of Wishes has about a 50% chance of rerolling a node into a Wish Fulfilled.
In wish nodes an extra option appears, guaranteeing a specific relic based on a priority list starting with Hand relics, then Books, then Golden Chalice (>=150 ingots), Coin Operated Toy (>=80 ingots), King's Relics (at 1 LP), and Sarkaz King's relics.

IS2.name: 'Beauty and Ugliness in the Age of Terra'
IS2.description:
For one time only during any battle prior to the final battle of the area, gain 10 Life Points and continue onto the next step instead of concluding the exploration upon mission failure.
When triggered, your current life total is preserved and you regain 10 life points. Best to trigger it by immediately quitting out to preserve your life total.

IS2.name: 'End of Times'
IS2.descriptionHtml: For one time only during any battle prior to the final battle of the area, gain 1 Life Point and continue onto the next step instead of concluding the exploration upon mission failure. When triggering the End of Times effect, your current life total is preserved, your run loss is averted, and you regain one life point. You do not receive the rewards that you would get if you win the fight (voucher, ingots etc.). If you lost by running out of lives, this means you will have 1 life point again. In contrast, if you have 10/12 life points on an IS3 run, and you use End of Times to instantly quit out on a non-boss stage, you will then have 11/12 life points. If you know you are going to trigger End of Times on a stage, it is best to trigger it by immediately quitting out (or doing so right before you leak your first enemy), in order to preserve your life total. End of Times will fail to trigger if you lose on a combat that is the last node of each floor, even if it isn’t the F3/F5/F6/F7 boss combat. This is only relevant in IS4 if you use a foldartal to reroll the f1 shop or f2/f4 wish fulfilled node into a combat, and is not relevant in either IS2 or IS3. Having End of Times and quitting out of a store rob combat will put you back in the store with all the items now free.

IS3.name: Fosterer Genotype
IS3.description:
An Operator with Rejection will assimilate 2 nearby normal Operators. When assimilation is complete, the Operator with Rejection temporarily gains [Evolution]: Rejection has no effect, gains 35% Sanctuary, attacks ignore 35% RES and DEF.
The effects of assimilation last for the stage it is triggered on only, it does not persist between different stages.
Operators that have assimilated a rejection cannot further spread the rejection. The effects of assimilation persist through retreats and redeploys; once an operator has assimilated a rejection in a given stage, there is no way to get rid of that rejection within that stage.
If you want to evolve an operator (say Mlynar) with a (natural) rejection, the two assimilated operators do not have to be in the 8 surrounding tiles of Mlynar at the same time, they can be placed within the vicinity of Mlynar sequentially, and Mlynar will become evolved after the 2nd operator has assimilated his rejection. Mlynar’s evolution progress will also be saved between deployments. If Mlynar assimilates 1 operator, then is retreated, then redeployed, then assimilates another operator, he will become evolved.
At D17, when rejections become combined rejections, the method of calculating operator redeployment times changes. At D15 and below, 2-star Yato with Metastatic Aberration has a redeployment time of (65 seconds*50%)-30 seconds = 2 seconds (rounded down), but with Metastatic Aberration in the form of a combined rejection, she has a (65-30)*50% = 17.5 seconds (unusually not rounded here) redeployment time. This applies to both natural rejections, and rejections obtained through assimilation. At specifically D16, for some reason, Metastatic Aberration (regardless of whether it is natural or assimilated) provides a -50% redeployment reduction still, but does not reduce operator stats.
Operators that gain Neurodegeneration from assimilating it will be frozen for the 15 seconds after they’ve gained the rejection, even if they’ve been deployed for longer than that.
A single operator can count towards assimilating the rejections of multiple operators, by deploying them on a tile where they are within the 8 surrounding tiles of both operators.

IS5.name: Arch Glyph
IS5.descriptionHtml:
For one time only, if you would fail any battle other than the final battle of the area or a Leader battle and are currently not in a Bizarre Fragment, you will enter a special Bizarre Fragment. When triggering the Arch Glyph effect, your current life total is preserved, your run loss is averted, and you regain one life point. You do not receive the rewards that you would get if you win the fight (voucher, ingots etc.). If you lost by running out of lives, this means you will have 1 life point again. In contrast, if you have 10/12 life points on an IS5 run, and you use Arch Glyph to instantly quit out on a non-boss stage, you will then have 11/12 life points. If you know you are going to trigger Arch Glyph on a stage, it is best to trigger it by immediately quitting out (or doing so right before you leak your first enemy), in order to preserve your life total. Arch Glyph will not save your run if you lose on a combat that is the last node of each floor, even if it isn't a boss combat. In IS5 this is only relevant if you reroll the F1 shop into a combat and lose to it, or on the last node of a Bizarre Fragment. Arch Glyph will trigger if you quit out while in a Face-Off node despite the fact that quitting out in a Face-Off node doesn't actually lose you the run. As long as you are in a non-boss/non-last node combat, the 'save your run' effect of Arch Glyph will always trigger, while the 'enter a Bizarre Fragment' effect will only trigger if you aren't already in a Bizarre Fragment. If you trigger Arch Glyph while already in a Bizarre Fragment on a node that isn't its last node, Arch Glyph will save your run, but not put you into another Bizarre Fragment. (If you lose on a combat that is the last node of a Bizarre Fragment, it will not save your run at all). If Arch Glyph is triggered while still inside a node (such as through robbing a store), the subsequent effects of that node will be resolved (for instance, you would be able to loot the shop) before you are brought to the Bizarre Fragment. (Like usual, you will not be able to receive the standard clear rewards for that combat.) If you use the Witchcraft thought to summon a portal, kill the portal, have the portal take away an operator, then quit out before the stage ends (even if the portal is the last enemy, you'll always have at least a few frames to quit out before the stage ends) the operator taken by the portal will immediately return in the Arch Glyph Bizarre Fragment. On the other hand, killing the Window summoned by Grand Wish, then popping Arch Glyph will not put you in the Epoch of Prosperity. Any damage dealt to the to the Finale Cadence (or killing it) will not properly be carried over if you end the stage by popping Arch Glyph. The Bizarre Fragment Arch Glyph brings you into always has the following layout. The encounter will always be the encounter 'Warm and Cozy,' at which you have the option to restore all your life points, or immediately leave:
The IS5 5th ending collectible 'Reality of the End' brings you to a special Bizarre Fragment that inevitably ends with the run's loss if you fail any stage. If you have both Arch Glyph and 'Reality of the End,' the one that you obtained first is the one that triggers first. This means that if you obtained Arch Glyph after already obtaining 'Reality of the End,' Arch Glyph cannot save you.

IS3.name: HR Bronze Seal
IS3.description:
Recruit a random 5-star Operator that is automatically Promoted.
You can reject the free operator offered to you.

IS5.name: HR Bronze Seal
IS5.description:
Recruit a random 5-star Operator that is automatically Promoted.
You can reject the free operator offered to you.

IS3.name: Doctor Silver Seal
IS3.description:
Recruit a random 6-star Operator that is automatically Promoted.
You can reject the free operator offered to you.

IS5.name: Doctor Silver Seal
IS5.description:
Recruit a random 6-star Operator that is automatically Promoted.
You can reject the free operator offered to you.

IS2.name: Victoria Crown
IS2.description:
All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Boss units gain an additional +15% ATK, +15% DEF, and +30% Max HP.
The stat modifier effects that apply to all enemies and those that apply to bosses are multiplicative with each other.

IS2.name: Half-Refined Diamond
IS2.description: All enemy units have +25% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Gain -50% Command EXP from battles.

IS3.name: 'Endless Life'
IS3.description:
Afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection at the start of each new floor.
This grants a rejection on the floor you receive it.

IS3.name: Wrankwood Safety Suit
IS3.description:
For each Cursed Curio you have, all allied units gain DEF +25%, RES +10.
Cursed Curios include both collectibles from F3 at D2 and above, and ending-related collectibles with negative effects (except Hesitation).

IS3.name: 'Flashing Swords'
IS3.description:
For each Cursed Curio you have, all allied units gain ASPD +35.
Cursed Curios include both collectibles from F3 at D2 and above, and ending-related collectibles with negative effects (except Hesitation).

IS5.name: Gul'dul's Silence
IS5.description:
All enemies gain +3000 DEF, but lose 100 DEF upon taking damage (Stacks up to 30 times).
Applied additively after EM buffs but before any external DEF multipliers.

IS5.name: Balor'sa a's Arrogance
IS5.description:
Elite and Leader enemies gain +20% HP, DEF, and ATK.
Multiplicative with other enemy external multipliers.

IS5.name: Yliš's Ravings
IS5.description:
All Operators start with -20 SP.
The sum of all positive and negative SP on deployment effects is calculated before the result is applied to the operator.

IS5.name: Shard of the Untold Kings
IS5.description:
Loadbearing Operators have -30% ATK.
The ATK reduction counts as an internal debuff instead of an external modifier.

IS3.name: Caerula Animus
IS3.description:
-50 Light; when obtained, immediately afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection, and leads the exploration toward a different ending.
You need at least 20 light to be able to get the encounter that gives you this.

IS3.name: 'Last Refrain'
IS3.description:
When obtained, randomly gain 2 Revelations.
The encounter for this collectible can only appear if you are on the path to 3rd Ending (i.e. you have the Caerula Animus).

IS4.name: Routeweave Net
IS4.description: Additional enemies appear in battle; leads expedition in a different direction.

IS4.name: Horizon Invitation
IS4.description:
Leads expedition in a different direction.
Obtaining this collectible gives a very high (but not 100%) chance that the next encounter will be the "Chance" encounter, where you can fight for Dimensional Fluid for 4th Ending.

IS4.name: Dimensional Fluid
IS4.description:
Leads expedition in a different direction.
After completing an ending fight, forces you onto the floor "Dust of Eternity" where you fight the 4th ending boss. To do 3rd and 4th Ending in a single run, you need to take Dimensional Fluid specifically on F6 Clairvoyant Carcass.

IS4.name: Amma's Affection
IS4.description:
All allied units gain +1% HP, ATK, and DEF; occasionally something wonderful will happen.
Unlocks special exchange options in several encounters, including trading it for 2 collectibles, 15 hope, or to skip a 4th ending requirement.

IS4.name: Fragment of Space
IS4.description:
-4 Squad Size Limit.
+squad size collectibles only grant the effect on pickup. Taking Fragment of Space after overshooting the 13 squad size hard cap will still bring you down to 9.

IS4.name: Profound Scorchmarks
IS4.description:
-12 Max Life Point.
Deliberately taking this is a reasonably good way of activating King's collectibles in IS4.

IS5.name: Ten Rings
IS5.description:
Shadows of the Lord -50% Max HP.
The encounter for this collectible is only obtainable if you have the Pledge of Babel.

IS5.name: Prophet Horn
IS5.description:
Nodes will turn into Prophecy when refreshed; not active in Bizarre Fragments.
The Prophet Horn reroll only works once.

IS5.name: Petal
IS5.description:
-50% Command EXP and Originium Ingots from battles; triggers wondrous effects in Face-Off.
After obtaining this, Face-Offs will have an option to expend 2 plans for the boats fight, available unlimited times until completed.

IS5.name: Anasa's Karma
IS5.description:
Leader and Elite enemies have +50% max HP; Dreadful Foes in Border of Truth will be more difficult; leads expedition in a different direction.
Also converts Emergency Lecture, Court Visit, Cognitive Correction and The Court of Souls to emergency versions with additional enemy stat buffs and Padmasana of Rebirth.
